_meta.purpose: P(target team commits MORE fouls than opponent) from odds-derived favorite_gap ONLY. Structural/universal (no team identity) -> transfers to any matchup incl. minnows via favorite_gap. Replaces the flat ~0.50 team_more_fouls placeholder.
_meta.convention: A = target team (the team named in 'will A commit more fouls'). favorite_gap = de-vigged A_win_prob - opp_win_prob (h2h). y=1 if A fouls strictly > opp; ties -> 0 (contest 'more than' resolves NO on tie).
_meta.fit_set: data/historical/stat_lines.csv club corpus, 168k team-rows, time-split train<2021 / test>=2021
_meta.oos_decomposition: ~70% of the beat is centering at true base rate 0.46 (not 0.50); ~30% is the favorite_gap tilt (dBrier vs base-rate-const -0.00067).
_meta.intl_sanity: StatsBomb WC2022 foul regime SAME as club (mean 14.06 vs 12.51, same shape) -> structural model applicable to internationals. Direction proxy inconclusive (no intl odds).
_meta.resolved_contest_rows: 11 real team_more_fouls rows: model net +19.89 vs flat-0.50, +22.52 vs crowd (closer 7/11). One heavy-favorite outlier (France +0.857 favgap, fouled MORE, -21.65) -> model is ROUGH, misses unusually-foul-prone favorites; net still positive.
_meta.ship: SHIP at k=1 (model IS the estimate, undistorted, like corners_cmp). Modest edge: 'stop flat-lining 120 rows with a grounded number', not 'beat the crowd big'. NOT yet wired into slate.py.
_meta.do_not: do not add team identity; do not moderate toward a placeholder (banned hedge); the model's own near-0.5 conservatism IS the moderation.
